sections.paragraph:
Κεγχρεαί, ῶν, αἱ (Thu. [Κεγχρειαί], X.+; edd. also Κενχρ-) Cenchreae, the seaport of Corinth (Philo, In Flacc. 155: Κ., τὸ Κορίνθιον ἐπίνειον) on the eastern side of the isthmus (Strabo 8, 6, 22) Ac 18:18; Ro 16:1; subscr. The port on the western side was Lechaeum (Diod S 11, 16, 3 ἀπὸ Λεχαίου μέχρι Κεγχρεῶν; 15, 68, 3).—WMichaelis, ZNW 25, 1926, 144–54; New Docs 3, 60 (coins); 4, 139f; BHHW II 939; Kl. Pauly III, 182.

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

edd. edd. = editions, editors

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

subscr. subscr. = subscription

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 537
